texto: It’s not what I think? If it looks like a duck and walks like a duck...” I imply with a shrug “...well then you know what they say.”“Nothing hap―”“Quack!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a K. Bromberg: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “It’s not what I think? If it looks like a duck and walks like a duck...” I imply with a shrug “..…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, huomor, men pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de men e relationship.

frase_original: It’s not what I think? If it looks like a duck and walks like a duck...” I imply with a shrug “...well then you know what they say.”“Nothing hap―”“Quack!

texto: Here comes the best part,” I say, realizing that I’ve spoken aloud the words I always tease Haddie for when she announces them at the bridge of the song. The lyrics come and I sing along as the words wash over me, moving me as they always do, bringing goose bumps to my flesh. “There you are, sitting in the garden, clutching my coffee, calling me sugar. You called me sugar.”“I don’t get it,” Colton says, “Why is that the best part?”“Because it’s the moment she realizes that he loves her,” I muse, a soft smile on my face.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de K. Bromberg que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Here comes the best part,” I say, realizing that I’ve spoken aloud the words I always tease Haddi…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre tempo, driven, i-race-you, com foco em i-race-you e race.

frase_original: Here comes the best part,” I say, realizing that I’ve spoken aloud the words I always tease Haddie for when she announces them at the bridge of the song. The lyrics come and I sing along as the words wash over me, moving me as they always do, bringing goose bumps to my flesh. “There you are, sitting in the garden, clutching my coffee, calling me sugar. You called me sugar.”“I don’t get it,” Colton says, “Why is that the best part?”“Because it’s the moment she realizes that he loves her,” I muse, a soft smile on my face.

texto: Things fucking change, dude! Life changes. Priorities change. Pre-fucking-conceived notions change. You have to adjust and change with them or your ass gets left behind.
explicacao: Orientação direta de K.Bromberg sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Things fucking change, dude! Life changes. Priorities change. Pre-fucking-conceived notions chang…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, change-your-life, change, útil em momentos de mudanca e reflexao.
